problem: What membrane material filters all contaminants from water at low energy cost?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) may resolve What membrane material filters all contaminants from water at low energy cost? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Prototype device testing with 7 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
